title: OECD Internship Programme
host_organization: Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development
funding: Monthly contribution to living expenses for interns located in Paris, plus leave according to OECD internship rules.
eligibility_criteria: Applicants must be enrolled in a fully accredited bachelor's, master's or doctoral degree programme for the full internship period, be available for at least one month, and have strong language, quantitative and IT skills relevant to OECD work.
field: Public policy, economics, data, education, climate, development, digital policy, governance, health, taxation, trade and related fields
duration: One to six months, renewable up to a total of twelve months; full-time placements are normally 40 hours per week.
benefits: Interns work with OECD policy experts and specialists, contribute to research and policy outputs, build analytical skills in an international environment, and may work in person, hybrid or remotely depending on team needs. The OECD states that interns located in Paris receive a contribution to living expenses and annual leave.
required_documents: Online application form, cover letter entered in the application platform, availability information, and any supporting documents requested by the OECD recruitment platform.
application_process: Applicants complete the online application through the official OECD SmartRecruiters platform, including application details, cover letter and availability. The Internship Office screens applications and shortlisted profiles are made available to OECD directorates. Hiring teams may invite candidates for an interview or written test before issuing an internship offer and agreement.
selection_criteria: Selection is based on fit with OECD directorate needs, academic background, relevant skills, language ability, analytical potential, availability and interview or written-test performance.
important_notes: The OECD welcomes interns on an ongoing basis and allows applications throughout the year. Candidates should apply well before their preferred start period because the selection process can take up to three months.
description:
The OECD Internship Programme is a verified international internship opportunity for students who want practical exposure to public policy, economics, governance and international cooperation. The programme is hosted by the Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development and brings students into policy and corporate teams that support evidence-based work with governments around the world. Interns may contribute to research, data collection, analysis, drafting, event support, communications and other project tasks depending on the directorate or unit that selects them.

This opportunity is especially useful for applicants who want to understand how international policy work is produced in practice. The OECD works across a wide range of themes, including climate change, digital transformation, education and skills, employment, finance, governance, health, trade, taxation, development and regional policy. A successful intern may help prepare studies and documents, review evidence, organise datasets, support publications or participate in meetings and seminars. The exact assignment depends on organisational needs, but the programme is designed to let motivated students build professional skills while contributing to the OECD mission of better policies for better lives.

Applicants must remain enrolled in a fully accredited degree programme during the whole internship. The official eligibility language includes bachelor's, master's and PhD students, and candidates are expected to have a strong command of at least one OECD official language, English or French, plus willingness or ability to work with the other. Quantitative, analytical and IT skills are valuable because many OECD teams use data, research methods and policy evidence in their daily work.

The application is submitted online through the OECD application platform. Candidates should prepare a focused cover letter that explains their academic background, policy interests, skills and availability. Because applications are considered on a rolling basis, there is no single fixed closing date. Applicants should still plan early: the OECD notes that selection can take up to three months, and candidates can update availability information after applying. The strongest applications will show a clear match between the applicant's studies and one or more OECD work areas.
seo_title: OECD Internship Programme 2026 | Paid International Policy Internship
seo_description: Apply for the OECD Internship Programme for students in policy, economics, data, climate, governance and related international fields worldwide now.

title: FAO Internship Programme
host_organization: Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations
funding: Internship conditions are set by FAO assignment calls; candidates should review the specific call on the FAO careers portal.
eligibility_criteria: Applicants must be nationals of FAO Member countries, be students in undergraduate or graduate programmes or recent graduates, have working knowledge of at least one FAO official language, normally be aged 21 to 30 at the start of the internship, and meet residence or visa requirements for the duty station.
field: Food security, agriculture, fisheries, forestry, nutrition, economics, statistics, communications, administration, international development and related fields
benefits: The programme gives selected students and recent graduates practical experience connected to FAO's mandate, Strategic Framework and the Sustainable Development Goals. Interns gain exposure to international development work, technical teams and multicultural collaboration while contributing skills relevant to food and agriculture.
required_documents: Online career portal application, motivation letter, evidence of attendance at a recognized university and any documents requested in the relevant Call for Expression of Interest.
application_process: Candidates apply online to the official FAO Call for Expression of Interest published on the FAO careers portal. The application should include a motivation letter and university attendance evidence. Only selected candidates are contacted, and candidates not selected before a call closes are asked to reapply to new calls if they want continued consideration.
selection_criteria: FAO considers educational background, relevance to FAO's mandate, language ability, age eligibility, adaptability, communication skills, basic computer skills and suitability for available assignments.
important_notes: The official FAO page explains that candidates apply through Calls for Expression of Interest on the career portal. Applicants should check the active call before submitting because duty stations, terms and closing dates can vary by call.
deadline: Varies by Call for Expression of Interest
description:
The FAO Internship Programme is a verified United Nations internship route for students and recent graduates who want practical experience in food, agriculture and sustainable development. It is hosted by the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations and is designed to connect academic learning with real programme work. FAO's mandate spans food security, nutrition, agricultural development, fisheries, forestry, climate resilience, rural transformation, data, policy and emergency response, so internship assignments may involve a wide range of technical and support areas.

The programme is intended for applicants who can contribute to FAO's Strategic Framework and the Sustainable Development Goals while learning from an international, multicultural workplace. Interns may support research, technical analysis, communications, programme operations, data work or administrative tasks depending on the office and assignment. Because FAO works through headquarters, regional offices and country offices, applicants should read each Call for Expression of Interest carefully to understand the duty station and assignment requirements.

Eligibility is specific and should be checked before applying. Candidates must be nationals of FAO Member countries and must either be enrolled in an undergraduate or graduate programme at a recognized institution or be recent graduates. They need working knowledge of at least one FAO official language, with a second official language considered an asset. FAO also indicates an expected age range of 21 to 30 at the start of the internship, plus residence or visa readiness for the assignment country. Candidates with close family members employed by FAO are not eligible.

The application process runs through the official FAO careers portal. Applicants should prepare a careful motivation letter, evidence of university attendance or graduation, and any additional documents requested in the active call. The best applications explain the connection between the applicant's academic background and FAO's work, show multicultural communication ability, and demonstrate readiness to contribute to practical assignments. Since calls can change, applicants should verify the exact closing date and assignment details on the career portal before submitting.
seo_title: FAO Internship Programme 2026 | UN Internship in Food and Agriculture
seo_description: Apply for the FAO Internship Programme for students and recent graduates in agriculture, food security and sustainable development fields worldwide.

title: World Food Programme Internship Programme
funding: Monthly stipend contribution up to US$1,000 depending on duty station, medical and accident insurance, official holidays, certified sick leave, visa support where applicable, and possible travel support for eligible nationals of developing economies studying in their home country.
eligibility_criteria: Applicants must be enrolled in an undergraduate or graduate programme and have attended classes in the past 12 months, or be recent graduates within six months who attended classes in the past 12 months. They must have completed at least two years of undergraduate studies, be fluent in English, be proficient in MS Office, and have no immediate relatives working for WFP.
field: Humanitarian assistance, food security, nutrition, data, logistics, supply chain, programme design, communications, finance, partnerships and related fields
benefits: Interns receive professional learning experience in humanitarian work, a stipend contribution, insurance coverage, access to training and e-learning resources, and exposure to WFP operations in country offices, regional bureaux and headquarters.
required_documents: Official online application, CV or profile documents requested by the WFP careers system, proof of student or recent graduate status, and any vacancy-specific documents.
application_process: Candidates review current internship vacancies through the official WFP careers platform and apply to relevant postings. Each posting should be checked for duty station, responsibilities, eligibility requirements, documents and closing date.
selection_criteria: WFP looks for academic fit, humanitarian motivation, English fluency, technical relevance, collaboration skills, MS Office proficiency and alignment with WFP behavioural competencies.
important_notes: WFP internship openings are advertised through the official WFP careers system. Deadlines vary by vacancy, so applicants must verify the closing date on the specific posting before applying.
description:
The World Food Programme Internship Programme is a verified humanitarian internship pathway for students and recent graduates who want practical exposure to food assistance, emergency response and sustainable development. WFP is the United Nations food assistance agency, and its internships are designed to complement academic study with professional assignments that support programmes, operations and technical teams. Interns may work in country offices, regional bureaux or headquarters depending on available vacancies and organisational needs.

The programme is suitable for applicants interested in humanitarian action, nutrition, food security, logistics, data, programme management, communications, partnerships, finance or related professional areas. Interns may conduct research and data analysis, draft documents and presentations, support project teams, contribute technical knowledge and collaborate across multicultural teams. The experience is intended to help young professionals understand how humanitarian organisations operate while giving WFP fresh perspectives and practical support.

WFP publishes clear eligibility rules. Applicants must be current undergraduate or graduate students who have attended classes in the past year, or recent graduates within six months who also attended classes in the past year. They must have completed at least two years of undergraduate study. English fluency is required, while knowledge of other UN languages can be an asset. Candidates should be comfortable with MS Office, collaborative work and cross-cultural communication, and they cannot have immediate relatives working for WFP.

The programme includes meaningful benefits. WFP states that interns may receive a stipend contribution linked to the city and country of assignment, not exceeding US$1,000 per month, along with medical and accident insurance during the internship. Depending on eligibility and assignment context, interns may also receive travel-related support, official holidays, certified sick leave, visa support and access to training resources. Because postings vary, applicants should read the active vacancy carefully and prepare a tailored application that connects their academic background to WFP's mission of ending hunger.
seo_title: World Food Programme Internship 2026 | WFP Humanitarian Internship
seo_description: Apply for WFP internships for students and recent graduates in humanitarian assistance, food security, logistics and global development roles now.
